verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a latent complex: the LAP (latency-associated peptide) remains non-covalently associated with the mature TGF-β1 dimer and the whole complex is covalently tethered to LTBP or GARP (LRRC32) / LRRC33 on the cell surface via disulfide bonds. GARP is a transmembrane protein that anchors the latent TGF-β1 complex to the outer leaflet of Tregs, platelets, and other cells via a covalent disulfide bond — making this a wash-resistant covalent attachment to a TM scaffold. This meets the 'stable_surface_attachment' criterion for contextual, as it is cell-type restricted (Tregs, platelets, certain stromal cells).
